title: Rhynchospora holoschoenoides
taxonomy: Rhynchospora holoschoenoides is a species of sedge, classified within the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This family comprises over 5,000 species, with many of them being aquatic or semi-aquatic plants. The genus Rhynchospora is further divided into several species, each with distinctive characteristics and growth habits. Rhynchospora holoschoenoides is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world, where it thrives in wetland environments. Its classification within the Cyperaceae family reflects its close evolutionary relationships with other sedges.
